w:r.w:t: This is to certify that the

by Om Gajjar(23162172002) of Ganpat University, towards the partial fulfillment of requirements of the degree of Bachelor of Technology – Computer Science and Engineering, carried out by them in the CSE(CS) Department at VUPAX Solutions Limited. The results/findings contained in this Project have not been submitted in part or full to any other University / Institute for award of any other Degree/Diploma.
Prof. Dhaval Sathawara & Signature of Internal Guide

IBM/Industry Internship project is a golden opportunity for learning and self-development. I consider myself very lucky and honored to have so many wonderful people lead me through in completion of this project. First and foremost, I would like to thank Dr. Rohit Patel, Principal, ICT, and Dr. Aniket Patel, Head, ICT who gave us an opportunity to undertake this project. My grateful thanks to Prof. Dhaval Sathawara & Mr. Anoj Dixit (Internal & External Guides) for their continuous guidance and support throughout the development of DukaanOS – Inventory Management System. Despite being extraordinarily busy with academics, they always took time to guide us and keep us on the correct path. We are truly grateful for their invaluable support. The CSE department monitored our progress and arranged all facilities to make life easier. We choose this moment to acknowledge their contribution gratefully.

The rapid growth of retail commerce in urban and semi-urban areas has created a strong demand for efficient and reliable inventory management solutions for small and medium-sized businesses. This project focuses on the development of a full-stack web application named "DukaanOS" – an Inventory Management System designed to help shopkeepers digitally manage their store operations.

The system provides a comprehensive set of features including OTP-based user authentication, multi-store management, category and item management, real-time stock tracking, billing with automated bill item computation, payment recording, and detailed audit logging. The backend is developed using the Django framework (Python) with a normalized MySQL database schema, while the frontend is implemented as a Single Page Application (SPA) using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

The primary contribution of this project involves designing a well-structured RESTful API backend, implementing a normalized multi-tenant database schema, and building an intuitive frontend interface that integrates seamlessly with backend services. The application emphasizes data integrity, security, and performance optimization to deliver a reliable and user-friendly experience for shopkeepers.

In today's fast-evolving retail environment, small and medium-sized shopkeepers face significant challenges in managing day-to-day store operations manually. Keeping track of inventory levels, recording sales, generating bills, and monitoring financial transactions through paper-based or rudimentary methods leads to frequent errors, stock discrepancies, and revenue losses. A digital solution that integrates all these operations into a single platform can significantly improve efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making for store owners.

system is a full-stack web-based Inventory Management System designed specifically for retail store owners. It enables shopkeepers to manage their items and categories, track real-time stock levels, generate itemized bills, record payments, and maintain comprehensive audit logs — all through an intuitive browser-based interface. The system follows a multi-tenant architecture where a single user can manage multiple stores, with
serving as the primary data isolation key across all tables.

The primary objective of this project is to develop a robust backend API layer and a responsive frontend interface that together deliver a seamless and reliable user experience. The backend is built using the Django framework (Python) with a normalized MySQL relational database, providing structured data management, data integrity, and secure API endpoints. The frontend is developed as a Single Page Application (SPA) using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, ensuring fast and interactive user interactions without full page reloads. The system also incorporates an OTP-based authentication mechanism using both phone number and email, adding a layer of security for user registration and login.
The scope of this project covers the end-to-end design and development of the

Inventory Management System. It includes designing a normalized relational database schema, developing RESTful backend APIs using Django, building a Single Page Application frontend, and integrating all system components into a functional web application.

The project encompasses the following core functional areas: user registration and authentication with dual-channel OTP verification (phone and email), store onboarding and profile management, product category and item configuration, stock addition and real-time inventory tracking, bill generation with automatic calculation of totals and tax, payment recording and transaction history, and a complete audit trail for stock changes, price updates, and user activity.

The individual contribution in this project is focused on full-stack development — encompassing database schema design, API development, frontend implementation, and system integration. Advanced functionalities such as cloud deployment, mobile application development, and third-party payment gateway integration are considered as part of the future roadmap and are not included within the current scope.
The development and execution of the
system require standard computing resources to ensure smooth performance during coding, testing, and deployment.
2.0 GHz or above (Intel Core i3 or equivalent)
Minimum 4 GB (8 GB recommended for running Django development server alongside MySQL)
At least 40 GB of free disk space for codebase, database, and dependencies
Minimum resolution of 1366 × 768 for proper UI development and testing
These hardware specifications are sufficient to handle backend development, database management, API testing, and local server execution without performance issues.
The project utilizes a combination of backend framework, database, frontend technologies, and development tools to build a complete web-based inventory management system.
Windows 10/11, Ubuntu Linux, or macOS (any OS supporting Python, MySQL, and modern web browsers)
Core programming language used for backend development
High-level Python web framework used for building RESTful API endpoints, URL routing, and ORM-based database interactions
Relational database management system used to store all application data including users, stores, items, stock, bills, payments, and audit records
Python MySQL database adapter used to connect Django with the MySQL database
Used for structuring the Single Page Application web interface
Used for styling, layout design, and responsive behavior of the frontend
Used for handling dynamic interactions, fetch API calls, DOM manipulation, and real-time UI updates
Primary code editor used for both backend and frontend development
Version control system for tracking code changes and collaboration
Used for testing and debugging RESTful API endpoints during development
Used for frontend testing, debugging, and verifying API responses through the browser console

A stable internet connection is required for OTP delivery via phone and email, as well as for API communication during testing. The system is designed to run on modern web browsers, ensuring compatibility across desktops, tablets, and mobile phones. MySQL must be installed and running locally for development and testing purposes.
approach, which provides a structured methodology for building reliable and efficient software systems. Each phase of SDLC ensures proper planning, execution, and validation of the application.
The stages followed in this project are:

In this phase, the system requirements were identified based on the practical needs of a retail store owner. Key features such as OTP authentication, multi-store management, item cataloguing, stock tracking, bill generation, payment recording, and audit logging were defined as functional requirements.

The database schema was planned to be fully normalized, defining tables for users, credentials, stores, categories, items, stock, stock_entries, bills, bill_items, payments, daily_metrics, and various audit tables. The API design was planned using RESTful conventions, and the frontend SPA layout was wireframed to ensure intuitive navigation.

The backend of the application was developed using Django and MySQL. Unmanaged Django models were written to map to the pre-existing MySQL schema without running Django migrations. The frontend SPA was built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, fetching data dynamically through fetch API calls to the Django REST endpoints.

The application was tested to ensure correct functionality of all API endpoints, proper data flow between frontend and backend, and validation of all user inputs. Edge cases such as duplicate registrations, expired OTPs, and invalid stock entries were tested and handled. Errors and inconsistencies were identified and resolved.

The application was prepared for execution in a local environment using Django's built-in development server alongside a locally hosted MySQL instance. The system is also designed to be compatible with deployment on cloud-based web platforms.
The full-stack development process was carried out in a structured manner to ensure smooth integration between the backend API layer and the frontend interface:
Designed a normalized multi-tenant MySQL database schema with proper foreign key relationships, indexing, and audit tracking capabilities.
Developed RESTful API endpoints in Django using function-based views to handle registration, OTP verification, login, store management, item CRUD, stock operations, billing, and payments.
Built the frontend interface as a Single Page Application that dynamically renders views and communicates with backend APIs using the Fetch API.
Verified API responses, form inputs, edge cases, and overall functionality of the integrated system using Postman and browser-based testing.
Improved the frontend interface by refining the layout, adding validation feedback, loading states, and improving overall user experience.

system was carried out through a series of structured activities covering database design, backend development, frontend implementation, API integration, and testing. The major activities performed during the project are as follows:

Designed and implemented the normalized MySQL database schema (dukaanos_schema_v2.sql) with all required tables, foreign key constraints, indexes, and default values. Set up the local MySQL environment and verified table relationships.

Developed the complete OTP-based registration and login flow covering phone and email dual-channel OTP verification, secure password hashing, session token management, and session expiration handling.

Implemented RESTful endpoints for store onboarding, product category creation, and item management (CRUD operations). Ensured store-level data isolation using store_id as the tenant key across all operations.

Developed stock management APIs to handle real-time stock additions, stock entry audit logs, and current stock level computation. Built the billing system with automatic bill item computation including GST calculation, total amount, and bill finalization.

Built the Single Page Application frontend that renders dynamic views for login, registration, dashboard, item listing, stock management, billing, and transaction history. Integrated all frontend components with the backend APIs using the Fetch API.
Conducted end-to-end testing of all API endpoints and frontend interactions. Resolved edge cases, improved error handling, added loading indicators, and refined the UI for a better user experience.
The project was completed over multiple phases from
, following an iterative development approach. Each phase involved continuous improvements, testing, and refinement of the system.
Database Schema Design & Setup – 3 to 4 days
Store, Category, Item & Stock API Development – 6 to 8 days
Billing & Payment System Development – 3 to 4 days
Frontend SPA Development & Integration – 5 to 7 days
Testing, Validation & Final Enhancements – 3 to 4 days
This structured timeline ensured efficient completion of the project while maintaining quality and performance.

The authentication system in DukaanOS was developed as a multi-step, OTP-based registration and login mechanism to ensure secure access to the platform. The system is built over the normalized schema tables: users, user_credentials, user_contact_verification, login_sessions, and a custom session_tokens helper table.
The registration flow is implemented across the following steps:

The register/check/ endpoint validates all user inputs including first name, last name, shop name, email, phone number, location, pincode, and password. Duplicate checks against user_credentials ensure no repeated registrations.

The otp/send/ endpoint sends a 6-digit OTP to both the user's phone number and email address simultaneously, storing SHA-256 hashed OTPs in the pending_registrations staging table with a defined expiry.

The otp/verify/register/ endpoint validates both OTPs. Upon successful verification, user records are atomically created across users, user_credentials, and stores tables, and a session token is issued.

The login flow uses a similar OTP-based verification (otp/verify/login/) with session token generation upon success. Bearer token authentication is enforced across all protected endpoints, with token expiry and session management handled through the session_tokens table. Password hashing uses Django's make_password and check_password utilities ensuring secure credential storage.

The system supports a multi-store architecture where a single registered user can own and manage multiple stores. Each store is uniquely identified by store_id, which acts as the tenant isolation key across all inventory-related tables.
Store details including store_name, location, pincode, GST number, and FSSAI number are stored in the stores table and are accessible via the /me/ endpoint after authentication.

The /categories/ endpoint supports creation and listing of item categories scoped to a specific store. Categories help organize the item catalog and facilitate structured inventory management. Individual categories can also be deleted through the /categories/<id>/delete/ endpoint.

Item and stock management form the core of the DukaanOS inventory system. The items table stores the product catalog for each store, while the stock table maintains the real-time inventory level for each item with current quantity, wholesale price, and selling price.

The /items/ endpoint supports creating, listing, updating, and deleting items with fields including item_name, brand, GST percentage, and category association. A compatibility shim (/products/) maintains backward compatibility with the frontend's existing field naming conventions.

The /items/<id>/stock/ endpoint processes stock additions, updating current_quantity and computing the running average of wholesale and selling prices. Each stock addition is recorded as an audit entry in the stock_entries table for complete traceability.
The frontend renders real-time item availability fetched dynamically from the backend, updating the UI without requiring a full page reload upon stock changes.

The billing system enables store owners to generate itemized bills for customer transactions. A bill is created with references to the store, individual items, quantities, and applicable GST rates, producing an accurate and complete transaction record.

The /bills/ endpoint processes new bills by creating entries in the bills table along with individual line items in the bill_items table. The system automatically computes subtotals, GST amounts, and grand totals based on item pricing and quantity.
Payment records associated with each bill are stored in the payments table, capturing payment amount, payment method, and timestamp for financial tracking.
Users can view the full list of past bills and payments from the frontend through the /stock-entries/ and /bills/ API endpoints, providing complete visibility into business transactions.

DukaanOS maintains a comprehensive audit trail to ensure data accountability and traceability across all major system operations. Dedicated audit tables record changes at a granular level, enabling store owners and administrators to review historical modifications.
Every stock addition is logged in the stock_entries table with the quantity added, price at the time of entry, and timestamp, providing a full history of inventory inflow.
Any modification to the selling price or wholesale price of an item is tracked in the audit_price_changes table, preserving the old and new values with timestamps.
User login events are recorded in login_sessions and audit_login tables, capturing IP address, device information, login time, and session status for security monitoring.
The daily_metrics table captures daily business performance data enabling future analytics and reporting capabilities.

The frontend of DukaanOS is implemented as a Single Page Application (SPA) using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, delivering a fast and interactive user experience without full page reloads. The application fetches data dynamically from the Django backend through the Fetch API and updates the DOM accordingly.

The SPA implements a view switching mechanism to render different sections — login, registration, dashboard, item list, billing, and transaction history — within the same HTML page, eliminating the need for separate HTML files for each route.
Bearer token is stored in browser session storage and included in all API request headers. The SPA handles token expiry and unauthorized responses by redirecting the user to the login view.
The frontend layout is built to be responsive across desktop, tablet, and mobile screen sizes, ensuring consistent usability for store owners regardless of their device.
User-friendly error messages are displayed for invalid inputs, failed API calls, and network errors, improving overall reliability and user trust in the system.

The DukaanOS project successfully demonstrates the design and development of a comprehensive full-stack Inventory Management System tailored for small and medium-sized retail stores. The application provides a reliable and intuitive platform for shopkeepers to manage their store operations digitally, covering the complete workflow from user authentication and inventory management to bill generation and financial record keeping.

The project demonstrates the importance of a well-designed, normalized database schema in ensuring data integrity and scalability in a multi-tenant system. The use of Django as the backend framework provided a clean and structured approach to API development, while the SPA-based frontend delivered a fast and responsive user experience. The OTP-based authentication system, comprehensive audit logging, and real-time stock management collectively make DukaanOS a dependable solution for retail store management.

Overall, this project highlights how effective full-stack development, structured database design, and seamless API integration play a crucial role in building practical and efficient digital solutions for real-world business problems.
The current system can be further enhanced by incorporating the following improvements:
Build dedicated Android and iOS mobile applications to provide store owners with on-the-go access to inventory, billing, and reports directly from their smartphones.
Implement a data analytics module that leverages daily_metrics data to provide visual reports on sales trends, top-selling items, revenue analysis, and stock movement patterns.
Introduce barcode scanning support for faster item lookup and stock addition, enabling integration with physical barcode scanners or smartphone cameras.
Deploy the application on a cloud platform such as AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure to support a larger user base, ensure high availability, and enable automated database backups.
Integrate payment gateway APIs to enable digital payment recording and reconciliation directly within the billing system, supporting UPI, card, and other popular payment methods.
